question_text: The electromagnetic spectrum includes radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, X-rays, and gamma rays. (a) Which two types are ionizing radiations and dangerous to human cells? (b) Which type is used for cooking food? (c) Order radio, visible light, and X-rays by frequency from lowest to highest.
correct_answer: (a) Ultraviolet, X-rays, and gamma rays are ionizing (any two correct). (b) Microwaves. (c) Radio waves, visible light, X-rays (lowest to highest frequency).
explanation: Ionizing radiations have enough energy to remove electrons from atoms, damaging DNA. UV causes sunburn and skin cancer; X-rays and gamma rays are more dangerous. Microwaves heat food by making water molecules vibrate. The electromagnetic spectrum is ordered by frequency: radio (lowest) to gamma rays (highest)—this is the reverse of wavelength order.
